results.guideline_title: A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible

explanation.summary: The pointer parameter 'msg' in the function 'log_message' should be const-qualified.
explanation.rule_basis: This code violates Rule 8.13 because a pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible. Inconsistent declarations and linkage decisions reduce maintainability and can break interface correctness.
explanation.code_evidence: The provided code snippet uses a non-const pointer 'msg' in the function 'log_message'.

fix_suggestions.title: Change the parameter type to const char*
fix_suggestions.why: By changing the parameter type to const char*, the pointer will be const-qualified, reducing the risk of unintended modifications.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This change ensures compliance with MISRA2012-RULE-8_13.
fix_suggestions.original_title: Change the parameter type to const char*

fix_suggestions.title: Make a local copy of the message before modifying it
fix_suggestions.why: By making a local copy of the message before modifying it, the original pointer remains const-qualified.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This change ensures compliance with MISRA2012-RULE-8_13, but it may increase memory usage.
fix_suggestions.original_title: Make a local copy of the message before modifying it

fix_suggestions.title: Use a const char[] for the log_buffer
fix_suggestions.why: By using a const char[] for the log_buffer, the pointer will be const-qualified.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This change ensures compliance with MISRA2012-RULE-8_13, but it may affect the behavior of the function if the log_buffer is modified elsewhere.
fix_suggestions.original_title: Use a const char[] for the log_buffer

risk_analysis.why: The use of a non-const pointer can lead to unintended modifications, which can break the interface correctness and reduce maintainability.
potential_failures: Data corruption due to unintended modifications
Interface correctness issues due to inconsistent declarations and linkage decisions

deviation_advice.recommended_decision: Implement one of the provided fix suggestions to ensure compliance with MISRA2012-RULE-8_13.
deviation_advice.required_justification: The use of a non-const pointer can lead to unintended modifications, which can break the interface correctness and reduce maintainability.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure that all pointers in the code are properly qualified as const or non-const to maintain consistency and reduce the risk of errors.

limitations: The warning text and retrieved rule meaning appear consistent.

results.guideline_title: The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used

explanation.summary: The return value of the 'puts' function is ignored, which violates MISRA2012-RULE-17_7.
explanation.rule_basis: The function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.
explanation.code_evidence: The code snippet provided returns the value of 'puts' but does not use it.

fix_suggestions.title: Assign the return value to a variable
fix_suggestions.why: To ensure the return value is checked for errors.
fix_suggestions.patched_code:
10      int status = strcpy(log_buffer, msg);
      11      status = puts(log_buffer);
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: The return value of 'strcpy' is now assigned to a variable.
fix_suggestions.original_patched_code:
10      int status = strcpy(log_buffer, msg);
      11      status = puts(log_buffer);
fix_suggestions.original_title: Assign the return value to a variable

fix_suggestions.title: Replace 'puts' with 'fprintf'
fix_suggestions.why: To provide more control over error handling.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: The 'puts' function is replaced with 'fprintf' which allows for more control over error handling.

fix_suggestions.title: Check the return value of 'puts'
fix_suggestions.why: To ensure the function call is successful.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: The return value of 'puts' is now checked for errors.

risk_analysis.why: The ignored return value could potentially lead to undetected errors.
potential_failures: Undetected errors due to ignored return value

deviation_advice.recommended_decision: Implement one of the suggested fixes.
deviation_advice.required_justification: Ignoring the return value of a function with a non-void return type violates MISRA2012-RULE-17_7.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure the return value is checked or handled appropriately.

limitations: The warning text and retrieved rule meaning appear consistent.

evaluation.summary: The return value of the 'puts' function is ignored, which violates MISRA2012-RULE-17_7.
evaluation.rule_basis: The function interfaces and calls must be explicit, safe, and consistently used.
evaluation.code_evidence: The code snippet provided returns the value of 'puts' but does not use it.

fixes.description: Assign the return value to a variable
fixes.compliance: The return value of 'strcpy' is now assigned to a variable.
The 'puts' function is replaced with 'fprintf' which allows for more control over error handling.
The return value of 'puts' is now checked for errors.
